title: Security Scan Results in Pipeline
dataSources: SAST (SonarQube, Checkmarx), DAST (ZAP, Burp), SCA (Snyk, Dependabot) results
spl:
index=cicd sourcetype="security_scan"
| stats count by severity, scan_type, project
| where severity IN ("Critical","High")
| sort -count
description: SAST/DAST/SCA findings in CI/CD pipelines catch vulnerabilities before they reach production. Tracking ensures security gates work.
value: SAST/DAST/SCA findings in CI/CD pipelines catch vulnerabilities before they reach production. Tracking ensures security gates work.
grandmaExplanation: SAST/DAST/SCA findings in CI/CD pipelines catch vulnerabilities before they reach production ‚Äî so code changes that fail, leak secrets, or skip checks are caught early.
implementation: Ingest security scan results from CI/CD pipelines. Track findings by severity, type, and project. Alert on critical findings blocking deployment. Report on vulnerability introduction rate and fix rate.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom scan result input.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: SAST (SonarQube, Checkmarx), DAST (ZAP, Burp), SCA (Snyk, Dependabot) results.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Ingest security scan results from CI/CD pipelines. Track findings by severity, type, and project. Alert on critical findings blocking deployment. Report on vulnerability introduction rate and fix rate.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cicd sourcetype="security_scan"
| stats count by severity, scan_type, project
| where severity IN ("Critical","High")
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Security Scan Results in Pipeline** ‚Äî SAST/DAST/SCA findings in CI/CD pipelines catch vulnerabilities before they reach production. Tracking ensures security gates work.

Documented **Data sources**: SAST (SonarQube, Checkmarx), DAST (ZAP, Burp), SCA (Snyk, Dependabot) results. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Custom scan result input. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cicd; **sourcetype**: security_scan. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=cicd, sourcetype="security_scan". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by severity, scan_type, project** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where severity IN ("Critical","High")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (findings by severity), Table (critical findings), Line chart (findings trend), Stacked bar (by scan type).
visualization: Bar chart (findings by severity), Table (critical findings), Line chart (findings trend), Stacked bar (by scan type).
